methylTracer 0.99.8
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Build methylation and coverage matrices

Source: R/build_count_matrix.R, R/build_count_matrix_SECURE.R
build_count_matrix.Rd

Process single-sample methylation bedGraph files into region-level methylation and coverage matrices. For each sample, the function (i) sorts and clips chromosomes, (ii) filters sites by coverage, (iii) removes excluded regions (e.g. SNPs, blacklist), (iv) maps per-base counts to the provided genomic annotation, and finally merges all samples into a methylation matrix and a coverage matrix, together with a processed annotation file (3.ann.bed).

Process single-sample methylation bedGraph files into region-level methylation and coverage matrices. For each sample, the function (i) sorts and clips chromosomes, (ii) filters sites by coverage, (iii) removes excluded regions (e.g. SNPs, blacklist), (iv) maps per-base counts to the provided genomic annotation, and finally merges all samples into a methylation matrix and a coverage matrix, together with a processed annotation file (3.ann.bed).

build_count_matrix(
  sam_info = NULL,
  input_dir = NULL,
  annotation_file = NULL,
  output_file = NULL,
  thresholds = 1,
  bedtools = NULL,
  bedSort = NULL,
  lib = "TAPS",
  exclude_bed = NULL,
  parallel = 1,
  genome_type = "human",
  limitFiles = 2e+05
)

build_count_matrix(
  sam_info = NULL,
  input_dir = NULL,
  annotation_file = NULL,
  output_file = NULL,
  thresholds = 1,
  bedtools = NULL,
  bedSort = NULL,
  lib = "TAPS",
  exclude_bed = NULL,
  parallel = 1,
  genome_type = "human",
  limitFiles = 2e+05
)

Arguments

sam_info

Character string. Path to a CSV file containing sample metadata. The file must contain a column named sample_name; its values must match the methylation file names in input_dir.

input_dir

Character string. Directory that contains all per-sample methylation files (one file per sample).

annotation_file

Character string. Path to a BED-like file defining genomic regions of interest (0-based), e.g. fixed windows, promoters, enhancers or CpG sites.

output_file

Character string. Base file name for the merged methylation matrix to be written in input_dir. The coverage matrix will be written to cov.<output_file> in the same directory.

thresholds

Numeric scalar. Minimum total coverage (\(methylated + unmethylated\) reads) required at a site before mapping it to regions.

bedtools

Character string. Path to the bedtools executable (version 2.30.0 or higher).

bedSort

Character string. Path to the UCSC bedSort executable (e.g. installed via conda install ucsc-bedsort=469).

lib

Character string specifying the library type. One of "TAPS" (directional conversion; default) or "WGBS" (indirect conversion). This controls how methylated and unmethylated counts are interpreted.

exclude_bed

Character vector of paths to BED files containing regions to be excluded (e.g. SNPs, blacklist). All regions are concatenated and merged before filtering.

parallel

Integer. Number of threads used by data.table when reading/writing text files.

genome_type

Character string. Genome assembly type, either "human" (default) or "mouse". Controls which chromosomes are retained.

limitFiles

Integer. Maximum number of file descriptors to allow when calling paste (used via ulimit -n).

Value

This function is called for its side effects. It writes three main files into input_dir:

  • <output_file>: merged methylation matrix (proportions 0–1).

  • cov.<output_file>: merged coverage matrix.

  • 3.ann.bed: processed genomic annotation BED file.

Details

Input methylation files are expected to be bedGraph-like text files with at least six columns: chr, start, end, and two columns of methylated and unmethylated counts. After filtering and mapping to the annotation, the function computes, for each region and each sample:

  • methylation proportion \(beta\) in the range \([0, 1]\), defined as \(beta = methylated / (methylated + unmethylated)\).

  • coverage as the total read count \(methylated + unmethylated\).

For each sample, intermediate files <sample>.meth (region-level beta) and <sample>.cov (region-level coverage) are written in input_dir. These are then merged into:

  • a methylation matrix: first column marker_name (chr_start_end), subsequent columns one per sample, values are methylation proportions in \([0, 1]\);

  • a coverage matrix with the same structure (cov.<output_file>).

The filtered and chromosome-restricted annotation is written to 3.ann.bed in input_dir.

Examples

## See vignette for a full end-to-end example.
## See vignette for a full end-to-end example.
